$comment: P2.5 smoke targets. Each entry pins a well-known TypeScript MCP server to a specific commit SHA from modelcontextprotocol/servers. Smoke script fetches via giget, runs `settlegrid add --dry-run --no-pr --json` against the subdir, and asserts the captured TransformOutput meets the expectations. Update the commit SHA when upgrading; other fields should rarely change.
$upstreamLicense: Apache-2.0 (verified 2026-04-15 by curl'ing LICENSE at the pinned SHA d5bfe349af1a3abc235e05cf6499d94400a10284 — note MCP is transitioning from MIT to Apache-2.0 so older commits may still be MIT). Both licenses are explicitly allowed by the P2.5 spec (`MIT/Apache-2.0`). Skipped `src/fetch`, `src/git`, `src/time` (Python) since our detection + transforms target TS/JS only.

targets.name: mcp-everything
targets.notes: modelcontextprotocol/servers#src/everything — larger reference server with server.server.setRequestHandler calls spread across resources/subscriptions.ts. Exercises the two-hop member expression matcher + init-fallback anchor for handler-only files.

targets.name: mcp-filesystem
targets.notes: modelcontextprotocol/servers#src/filesystem — single-file MCP server built on McpServer + the higher-level `server.tool(...)` API. Exercises the 'new McpServer() but no setRequestHandler' path — import + init land at the top of the entry file, no handler wraps get emitted.

targets.name: mcp-memory
targets.notes: modelcontextprotocol/servers#src/memory — small single-file MCP server with McpServer + tool registrations. Third independent repo shape in the smoke suite.
